title: The Documents in the Case
description: The Documents in the Case — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: 15 Whittington Terrace
memes.description: London residence in Bayswater where Agatha Milsom and John Munting lived as neighbors, serving as a key setting for the epistolary narrative of The Documents in the Case.

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Documents in the Case" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Milsom's Letter to Elizabeth Drake"
memes.description: Agatha Milsom's rare letter to Elizabeth Drake (Munting's fiancée) — a cross-correspondence bridging two epistolary threads

memes.label: Agatha Milsom
memes.description: Primary letter writer in the epistolary narrative; a perceptive spinster living at 15 Whittington Terrace, Bayswater, who corresponds with Olive Farebrother about the Harrison household

memes.label: "Amanita muscaria (Fly Agaric)"
memes.description: The Fly Agaric mushroom — Amanita muscaria — the iconic red-capped fungus whose toxic alkaloid muscarine was extracted and used to murder George Harrison; central to the forensic botany of The Documents in the Case

memes.label: Amanita Phalloides
memes.description: A deadly poisonous mushroom species, distinct from Amanita muscaria, relevant to the toxicological context of the case.

memes.label: Autopsy of George Harrison
memes.description: The post-mortem examination of George Harrison performed by Dr. Hughes of Bovey Tracey, which established the cause of death and provided key medical evidence.

memes.label: "Bovey Tracey"
memes.description: A market town in Devon, England — the administrative hub for the George Harrison murder investigation, site of the inquest, police station, autopsy, and a key railway junction on the route to The Shack

memes.label: Brimley Halt
memes.description: A railway halt on the line between Newton Abbot and Bovey Tracey in Devon, relevant to Lathom's movements on the day of the murder

memes.label: Central Africa
memes.description: The region where Paul Harrison was working as a civil engineer, building a bridge during the events of the case

memes.label: "35: Telegram from John Munting to Elizabeth Drake, Dated 26.2.29"
"47: Extract from the Morning Express of Tuesday, October 21st, 1929"
"48: Extract from the Morning Express of Wednesday, November 5th, 1929"
"Chemical Analysis"
memes.description: The forensic technique for identifying and distinguishing chemical compounds — central to the Harrison case because the distinction between natural and synthetic muscarine could only be established through chemical analysis

memes.label: "Chemical Analysis Report"
memes.description: The Home Office chemical analysis of George Harrison's organs and stomach contents, conducted by Sir James Lubbock during the adjourned inquest — the foundational forensic document that identified synthetic muscarine as the cause of death and transformed the Harrison case from a suspected natural death into a confirmed homicide

memes.label: "Christmas 1928 at Whittington Terrace"
memes.description: The Christmas season when Harwood Lathom joined the Harrison household at 15 Whittington Terrace — the beginning of the events leading to murder

memes.label: The Circumstantial Case
memes.description: The nature of the evidence in the Harrison case — entirely circumstantial, built from documents, chemical analysis, and inference rather than direct testimony or confession.

relations.label: divorce was not a viable option

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: Index of all concepts and thematic topics explored in The Documents in the Case.

memes.label: "Death of George Harrison"
memes.description: The murder of George Harrison at The Shack, Manaton, Devon, by muscarine poisoning — the central crime of the narrative

memes.label: The Divorce Question
memes.description: The barrier that George Harrison's existence posed to Margaret Harrison and Harwood Lathom's relationship — with divorce being socially ruinous and legally slow, making it an impossible solution.

memes.label: "Dorothy L. Sayers"
memes.description: Dorothy L. Sayers (1893–1957) — English crime writer, translator, and co-author with Robert Eustace of The Documents in the Case; creator of Lord Peter Wimsey

memes.label: "Dr. Hughes"
memes.description: Physician of Bovey Tracey who performed the autopsy on George Harrison and gave key medical evidence at the inquest

memes.label: Dr. Pringle
memes.description: The coroner for the district who presided over the inquest into George Harrison's death.

memes.label: Dr. Trevor
memes.description: Agatha Milsom's psychoanalyst, whose Freudian concepts and therapeutic advice profoundly shape her interpretation of the Harrison household dynamics

memes.label: Dr. Waters
memes.description: A chemist present at the final dinner party who explained the principles of synthetic versus natural muscarine to Paul Harrison.

memes.label: Elizabeth Drake
memes.description: Fiancée of John Munting, nicknamed "Bungie"; recipient of his letters throughout the novel and occasional correspondent herself

memes.label: "Drake's Letter to Munting"
memes.description: Elizabeth Drake's reply to John Munting — the only letter from her in the dossier, providing her perspective on the unfolding events

memes.label: Epistolary Form
memes.description: The literary technique of telling a story through letters and documents — the defining narrative structure of The Documents in the Case.

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Chronological index of all key events in The Documents in the Case.

memes.label: Evidence Index
memes.description: Complete index of all evidence documents — letters, telegrams, statements, and reports — in The Documents in the Case.

memes.label: The Execution at Exeter Gaol
memes.description: The execution of Harwood Lathom at Exeter Gaol, the final event of the case and the culmination of the criminal proceedings.

memes.label: "Five-Acre Wood"
memes.description: A wood near The Shack, Manaton, known locally as a productive site for toadstools and fungi — the likely source of the mushrooms used in the fatal dish

memes.label: "The Forensic Investigation"
memes.description: The forensic investigation led by Sir James Lubbock that identified muscarine poisoning and transformed the Harrison death into a murder case

memes.label: George Harrison
memes.description: The murder victim at the center of The Documents in the Case — Head of the Accountants' Department, aged 56, living at 15 Whittington Terrace, Bayswater; father of Paul Harrison and husband of Margaret Harrison; died of synthetic muscarine poisoning at The Shack in Manaton, Devon

memes.label: "George Harrison's Letters to Paul"
memes.description: George Harrison's letters to his son Paul, written from 15 Whittington Terrace; document the household dynamics before his death

memes.label: George Harrison's Watercolours
memes.description: George Harrison's artistic hobby of painting watercolours of plants and fungi in their natural habitat — a pastime that provides insight into his character and becomes a narrative thread in the correspondence.

memes.label: Handwriting Evidence
memes.description: The role of handwriting analysis in the Harrison case — Paul Harrison's investigation into forged documents in his stepmother's handwriting.

memes.label: Harold Coffin
memes.description: A labourer from Manaton, Devon, who gave evidence at the inquest about seeing George Harrison gathering mushrooms on the morning before his death

memes.label: Harwood Lathom
memes.description: A brilliant young artist who boards at the Harrisons' maisonnette in Bayswater; his expertise in mycology and chemistry, combined with his affair with Margaret Harrison, makes him the prime suspect in the murder of George Harrison by synthetic muscarine poisoning

memes.label: Hedgehog Cookery
memes.description: George Harrison's unconventional culinary practice of cooking and eating hedgehogs, broiled over the fire — a recurring motif in the correspondence that illustrates his eccentricity.

memes.label: Home Office
memes.description: The British government department whose analyst, Sir James Lubbock, provided forensic testimony at the George Harrison inquest

memes.label: "I to Hercules"
memes.description: A novel by John Munting that became an unexpected bestseller — the work that established his public identity as "author of I to Hercules" and whose success formed a subplot within the documentary record

memes.label: "Coroner's Inquest Testimony"
memes.description: The compiled testimony from the coroner's inquest into George Harrison's death, including evidence of identification and the widow's appearance

memes.label: Jack Munting
memes.description: John Munting's brother, mentioned in Munting's letters to Elizabeth Drake

memes.label: Sir James Lubbock
memes.description: Home Office Analyst who conducted the chemical analysis of George Harrison's remains and identified muscarine poisoning as the cause of death

memes.label: John Munting
memes.description: A chemist who writes to his fiancée Elizabeth Drake; lives in the upstairs flat at 15a Whittington Terrace and provides key forensic testimony

memes.label: "John Munting's Statement"
memes.description: John Munting's formal statement providing a chemist's perspective on the events leading to George Harrison's death; contains the critical demonstration that synthetic muscarine can be produced through simple laboratory methods and is chemically indistinguishable from the natural product

memes.label: "Arrest of Harwood Lathom"
memes.description: The arrest of Harwood Lathom for the murder of George Harrison, following the inquest's forensic findings

memes.label: "Lathom's Letter to Munting"
memes.description: Harwood Lathom's sole letter in the dossier, addressed to John Munting — a rare direct document from the prime suspect

memes.label: Lustleigh Cleave
memes.description: A valley and tor near Manaton, Devon, visible from The Shack and mentioned in Harwood Lathom's letters

memes.label: "Manaton"
memes.description: A remote village in Devon, England, near The Shack — site of the resumed inquest, the local post-office, and the communication hub for the isolated cottage where George Harrison died

memes.label: Margaret Harrison
memes.description: The wife of George Harrison whose affair with Harwood Lathom and ambiguous role in her husband's death make her one of the most complex figures in the case.

memes.label: "Margaret Harrison's Letters to Lathom"
memes.description: Margaret Harrison's letters to Harwood Lathom, including the letter that "clearly indicated the affair was over" — key evidence of their relationship

memes.label: Margaret Harrison's Letters to Lathom
memes.description: Margaret Harrison's intimate letters to Harwood Lathom, written between May and September 1929, revealing their adulterous affair and her desire to be with him

memes.label: "The Morning Express"
memes.description: A daily newspaper that published three key extracts documenting the George Harrison case — the primary contemporary press source for the documentary record

memes.label: "Morning Express: Conviction Report (Nov 30, 1930)"
memes.description: The Morning Express report of November 30, 1930, recording the conviction for the murder of George Harrison at The Shack

memes.label: "Morning Express: Inquest Report (Oct 21, 1929)"
memes.description: The Morning Express newspaper report on the opening of the inquest into George Harrison's death, published October 21, 1929

memes.label: "Morning Express: Lubbock Testimony (Nov 5, 1929)"
memes.description: The Morning Express report on Sir James Lubbock's forensic testimony identifying muscarine poisoning as the cause of George Harrison's death

memes.label: Morning Express Lubbock Testimony
memes.description: A testimony or report published in the Morning Express newspaper concerning the Lubbock aspect of the Harrison case.

memes.label: Most Referenced Pages
memes.description: The most cross-referenced pages in the wiki, ranked by total backlink count.

memes.label: Motive in the Harrison Case
memes.description: Analysis of the possible motives for the murder of George Harrison — including romantic jealousy, financial interest, and the escape from an intolerable marriage.

memes.label: Mr. Perry
memes.description: The vicar mentioned in Agatha Milsom's letters; an admirer of her needlework and a minor figure in the Norfolk social circle

memes.label: Mrs. Cutts
memes.description: The charwoman at The Shack in Manaton, Devon; provides tea and incidental testimony during the investigation of George Harrison's death

memes.label: "Munting Discovers the Poison Evidence"
memes.description: The moment John Munting, drawing on his chemical knowledge, recognized the forensic significance of Lathom's mushroom expertise and the synthetic poison conversation

memes.label: "Munting and Lathom as Flatmates"
memes.description: The period when John Munting and Harwood Lathom shared the ground-floor maisonnette at 15a Whittington Terrace — a friendship that complicated the investigation
aliases: Munting and Lathom share the maisonnette

memes.label: "Muscarine Poisoning"
memes.description: The method of murder in The Documents in the Case — deliberate poisoning via muscarine, a toxic alkaloid extracted from the Amanita muscaria mushroom, administered through a prepared fungus dish

memes.label: Mushroom Identification
memes.description: The skill and risks of identifying wild mushrooms for consumption — the practice that led to George Harrison's death and the central ambiguity between accident and murder.

memes.label: Natural vs. Synthetic Poison
memes.description: The forensic distinction between naturally occurring poisons (extracted from organisms) and synthetic poisons (produced in a laboratory) — the key chemical insight in the Harrison case.

memes.label: Neglected Edible Treasures
memes.description: George Harrison's book about edible wild foods, published by Messrs. Hopkin & Bigelow, which was referenced at the inquest

memes.label: Newspaper Coverage of the Harrison Case
memes.description: The role of newspaper reporting in the Harrison case — how the Morning Express coverage of the inquest and trial shaped public perception and preserved the official record.

memes.label: "Newton Abbot"
memes.description: A larger market town in Devon, England — the mainline railway junction where passengers changed for the Bovey Tracey branch line en route to The Shack, Manaton

memes.label: Olive Farebrother
memes.description: Primary recipient of Agatha Milsom's letters; her off-stage presence as confidante enables the epistolary narrative

memes.label: "Paris Trip (December 1928)"
memes.description: George and Margaret Harrison's trip to Paris, December 15-28, 1928 — their last holiday together before the murder

memes.label: Paul Harrison
memes.description: Son of George Harrison by an earlier marriage; a civil engineer who investigates his father's death and compiles the documentary evidence

memes.label: Paul Harrison in Central Africa
memes.description: Paul Harrison's work as a bridge-building engineer in Central Africa — the reason for his absence from England during the critical events and his return to investigate his father's death.

memes.label: "Paul Harrison's Editorial Notes"
memes.description: Paul Harrison's editorial notes framing the documentary evidence; his annotations provide context and chronology for the assembled letters and statements

memes.label: "Paul Harrison Returns from Africa"
memes.description: Paul Harrison's return from his civil engineering posting in Africa to investigate his father's death and compile the documentary evidence

memes.label: "Paul Harrison's Statement"
memes.description: Paul Harrison's comprehensive statement compiling the documentary evidence against Harwood Lathom for the murder of George Harrison

memes.label: Person Index
memes.description: Complete index of all persons and characters referenced in The Documents in the Case.

memes.label: Place Index
memes.description: Index of all places and locations mentioned in The Documents in the Case.

memes.label: Professor Alcock
memes.description: An old friend of George Harrison, cited by Harrison as an authority against Einstein's theories

memes.label: Professor Hoskyns
memes.description: A brilliant physicist and old Oxford contemporary of Mr. Perry, who appears at a dinner gathering in the final chapter

memes.label: Professor Matthews
memes.description: A biologist specializing in heredity, Oxford contemporary of Mr. Perry, nicknamed "Stingo"

memes.label: Psychoanalysis
memes.description: The psychological framework implicitly referenced in character motivations and behaviors throughout The Documents in the Case.

memes.label: Robert Eustace
memes.description: Pseudonym of Eustace Robert Barton, a medical doctor and co-author with Dorothy L. Sayers on The Documents in the Case.

memes.label: Royal Academy
memes.description: The Royal Academy of Arts in London, where Harwood Lathom exhibited his portrait of Margaret Harrison in 1929

memes.label: Sergeant Warbeck
memes.description: Police sergeant at Bovey Tracey who receives the report of George Harrison's death and assists in the initial investigation at The Shack

memes.label: Sir Gilbert Pugh
memes.description: The Director of Public Prosecutions who reviewed Paul Harrison's manuscript account of the case

memes.label: Sir Maurice Berkeley
memes.description: Distinguished engineer, Paul Harrison's chief, who was building a bridge in Central Africa during the events of the case

memes.label: Stereochemistry
memes.description: The branch of chemistry dealing with the three-dimensional arrangement of atoms in molecules — the scientific principle that allowed the distinction between natural and synthetic muscarine.

memes.label: Synthetic Muscarine
memes.description: Laboratory-produced muscarine in its racemic form, distinguished from the natural alkaloid found in Amanita muscaria — the key forensic distinction that unraveled the Harrison case.

memes.label: "Telegram: Munting to Drake (Feb 26, 1929)"
memes.description: The critical telegram from John Munting to Elizabeth Drake dated February 26, 1929 — a key timeline marker in the murder investigation

memes.label: "The Affair: Margaret Harrison and Harwood Lathom"
memes.description: The clandestine relationship between Margaret Harrison and Harwood Lathom that developed during February 1929 — the motive for murder

memes.label: "Conviction of Harwood Lathom"
memes.description: The conviction of Harwood Lathom for the murder of George Harrison — the final resolution documented in the Morning Express of November 30, 1930

memes.label: "The Documents in the Case (Novel)"
memes.description: The Documents in the Case — the 1930 collaborative epistolary detective novel by Dorothy L. Sayers and Robert Eustace, presented as a dossier of letters, statements, and documents compiled by Paul Harrison to investigate his father's death

memes.label: "The Dossier: Complete Documentary Record"
memes.description: The complete collection of letters, statements, telegrams, and newspaper extracts compiled by Paul Harrison into the documentary dossier that forms the novel
aliases: The Documents in the Case dossier

memes.label: "The Coroner's Inquest"
memes.description: The coroner's inquest into the death of George Harrison, opened by Dr. Pringle on October 20, 1929, and adjourned for a fortnight to enable chemical analysis — the proceeding that transformed a suspected natural death into a murder investigation

memes.label: "The Mushroom Dinner Party"
memes.description: A dinner party at 15 Whittington Terrace, Bayswater, early in 1929, at which Harwood Lathom prepared his "special mushroom dish with his own fair hand" — a culinary performance that, in retrospect, demonstrated both the means and the opportunity for the murder method later employed against George Harrison

memes.label: "The Shack, Manaton"
memes.description: A small cottage near Manaton, Devon, where George Harrison died of muscarine poisoning during a weekend gathering — the primary crime scene of the case

memes.label: "The Shack Weekend"
memes.description: The fatal weekend at The Shack, Manaton, Devon, where George Harrison died after eating mushrooms prepared by Harwood Lathom

memes.label: "The Telegram (February 26, 1929)"
memes.description: The sending of the cryptic telegram from John Munting to Elizabeth Drake on February 26, 1929 — a pivotal moment of realization

memes.label: "The Trial of Harwood Lathom"
memes.description: The trial of Harwood Lathom for the murder of George Harrison; the legal proceedings that concluded with his conviction

memes.label: Timeline of Events
memes.description: Chronological timeline of all key events in The Documents in the Case, from December 1928 through the trial and aftermath

memes.label: Warty Caps
memes.description: Amanita rubescens, an edible mushroom known as Warty Caps, which closely resembles the poisonous Fly Agaric (Amanita muscaria)

memes.label: "15 Whittington Terrace, Bayswater"
memes.description: The Harrison family home at 15 Whittington Terrace, Bayswater, London — the domestic stage where the relationships, tensions, and preparations leading to George Harrison's murder unfolded
